title: Rank-Ensembled Categorical Hazard Models
summary: Current best appears feature-engineering-heavy, likely with one dominant tabular learner.
rationale: Current best appears feature-engineering-heavy, likely with one dominant tabular learner. CatBoost’s ordered categorical handling and monotonic/regularized GBMs can capture different biases for high-cardinality Driver/Race interactions and physically monotone tyre-wear effects.
implementation_hint: Train several fold-consistent models on the same engineered table: CatBoost with native Driver/Race/Compound and combinations; LightGBM/XGBoost with monotone constraints on TyreLife, degradation, old-tyre ratios, and laps-remaining directions; optionally ExtraTrees for nonlinear diversity. Blend OOF-tuned rank-normalized predictions rather than raw probabilities.
expected_effect: May gain small but reliable AUC by reducing model-specific ranking errors without requiring new labels or full script rewrite.
risk: Monotone constraints can over-restrict valid cases such as late-race no-stop decisions; tune constrained models as ensemble members, not replacements.
